title: Identidade Nostr canônica e RBAC persistido no backend

artifacts.reference: 77 passed with PostgreSQL 16 ephemeral; 9 targeted RBAC PostgreSQL tests passed
single head 0015_identity_rbac with parent 0013_review_and_obligation; partial community migration parked outside discovery
user_roles, company_memberships, canonical pubkey constraint, four mutation-protection triggers
two runs with fictitious public-only pubkeys yielded one membership and canonical role sets
OpenAPI organization company boundary and existing participant/admin authentication routes

positive_results: Alembic reported exactly one head, 0015_identity_rbac, and a fresh PostgreSQL database upgraded from base through that revision.
Participant, organization and administrative identities received the expected 200 or 403 responses for their route boundaries.
An organization could access its own company but not a different company.
Payloads containing role, roles or session_scope received 422 and created no privileged grant.
The runtime database principal could read authorization state but could not grant, update, delete or truncate roles.
The owner-only seed was idempotent and role revocation remained auditable and one-way.
The parked partial migration remains preserved outside Alembic discovery and no apps/web path was changed by this run.
limitations: Self-verified evidence in a dirty workspace does not satisfy the S2 acceptance gate.
Remote Supabase connectivity was not tested because apps/api/.env.local has no configured PostgreSQL connection string.
No real pubkeys were seeded; only fictitious public values were used in the disposable database.
No browser extension or real NIP-07 prompt was exercised in the new PostgreSQL RBAC tests.
No migration, seed, write or schema inspection was executed against Supabase.
This evidence authorizes no external effect.
